paths.comment: Baseline contains 'HeadlessChrome/147.0.0.0' from the bare Chromium-for-Testing capture; consistency engine R-004 rebuilds a clean UA from primitives, dropping the leak. The diff is intentional — the spoof is more correct than the baseline. PLAN.md §9.2 (R-004); tasks/0050-harness-mvp.md.
Same root cause as navigator.userAgent — derived from the cleaned UA via R-026 (appVersion = userAgent without the leading 'Mozilla/'), so it likewise omits HeadlessChrome.

Bare Chromium reports the exact tip patch version (147.0.7727.138 at capture time). The consistency engine produces a deterministic-but-seed-derived patch from R-023's build-hash, which won't match the device's tip. v0.7 may pin to the device's exact patch; v0.5 treats this as intentional.
navigator.connection.effectiveType is per-network-link and not yet covered by the consistency engine. v0.7 may add a network-class rule. docs/limits.md.
Chrome's published deviceMemory is capped at 8; the baseline shows 32 (from a non-Chrome JS path or device override). The consistency engine emits the canonical capped value via R-008. v0.7 polish.
Permissions API state is not yet covered (PLAN.md §9.6 — known limit; docs/limits.md "Notification.permission and Permissions.query"). v0.7 polish.
Per-session storage usage; varies per run. Treated as intentional rather than guid-class because the value is a small int, not a sentinel-able string.
Boolean for Bluetooth API surface. Not yet covered by the consistency engine; depends on device vendor + Chrome flags. docs/limits.md, phase 0.7.
Boolean for WebUSB API surface. Same status as navigator.bluetooth.
Boolean for Web Serial API surface. Same status as navigator.bluetooth.
Boolean for WebHID API surface. Same status as navigator.bluetooth.
Boolean for WebXR API surface. Same status as navigator.bluetooth.
Boolean for WebGPU API surface; corresponds to the WebGPU info block, which is the explicit phase-0.7 deliverable per docs/limits.md.
Boolean for Ink API surface. Phase 0.7 polish.
Boolean for Scheduling API surface. Phase 0.7 polish.
Boolean for Window Controls Overlay API. Phase 0.7 polish.
Boolean for Virtual Keyboard API. Phase 0.7 polish.
Bot-globals module strips webdriver to false; baseline captures it as true (bare Chromium-for-Testing reports webdriver). The diff is intentional — the spoof is correct.
Inject lays down screen.* properties from the matrix's display.dpr; bare capture from a different DPI display can differ. Profile-driven, not yet seed-stable.
Media-query results depend on OS reduce-motion / contrast / color-scheme system settings at capture time; the consistency engine doesn't yet model these. Phase 0.7 polish.
Orientation is screen-derived; not yet locked by the consistency engine. Phase 0.7 polish.
Canvas hash + dataUrl are deferred to phase 0.7 (audio bytes + canvas hash maps). docs/limits.md "Canvas fingerprinting (HTMLCanvasElement.toDataURL) is NOT spoofed at v0.3".

Full WebGL surface (extensions catalog, precisionFormats, max* counters) is deferred to phase 0.7 — only the curated R-024 vendor/renderer/maxTextureSize/maxColorAttachments are spoofed at v0.5. docs/limits.md "WebGPU adapter info is NOT spoofed at v0.3" applies similarly to the WebGL extension catalog.
WebGPU adapter info is explicitly deferred to phase 0.7. docs/limits.md "WebGPU adapter info is NOT spoofed at v0.3".

Audio fingerprint bytes (sampleValues, audioHash) are deferred to phase 0.7 — requires precomputed per-(profile, sample-rate) byte tables. docs/limits.md "Audio fingerprinting (OfflineAudioContext) is NOT spoofed at v0.3".
MediaDevices.enumerateDevices spoofing is deferred to phase 0.7 — needs persistent (profile, seed)-bound device list. docs/limits.md "MediaDevices.enumerateDevices() is NOT spoofed at v0.3".
SpeechSynthesis.getVoices spoofing is deferred to phase 0.7 — needs per-OS voice catalog. docs/limits.md "SpeechSynthesis.getVoices() is NOT spoofed at v0.3".
Full per-device font lists are deferred to phase 0.7 — only the curated R-013 system-pack subset is spoofed at v0.5. The hash + count + tested fields all depend on the full list.
Storage API surface (sharedArrayBuffer, broadcastChannel, openDatabase, fileSystemAccess, sharedWorker, etc.) — not yet locked by the consistency engine. Phase 0.7 polish.

Intl-derived timing surface (supportedCalendars, supportedNumberingSystems, supportedTimeZones, supportedUnits, dateString, localeString, mathFingerprint, etc.) — these are stock V8 outputs that the consistency engine cannot override from JS. Phase 0.7 audit; v0.5 treats them as intentional. The Intl primitives are deterministic per Chrome version, not per-device, so they don't actually leak device class.

Bot-detection helpers in the probe page expose webdriver, headlessChrome, chromeRuntime, plugin counts, etc. The bot-globals inject module covers webdriver/chromeRuntime; some sub-fields (e.g. bot.headlessChrome derivation, bot.userActivation, bot.pluginCount) are downstream of larger phase-0.7 surfaces (plugins, navigator.webdriver). Bucket as intentional pending phase 0.7.
